hints.title: Compare join and leave
hints.content: Walk through what `join()` does and what `leave()` does. They should be symmetric in terms of resources allocated and released — they aren't.

hints.title: EventEmitter is explicit
hints.content: Node's EventEmitter does not auto-remove listeners when an unrelated object is garbage collected. Listeners stay until you call `off()` / `removeListener()`.

hints.title: Keep a handle on the handler
hints.content: To remove a listener you need the same function reference you passed to `on()`. Store the handler somewhere keyed by userId so leave() can look it up.

hints.title: Solution outline
hints.content:
1) Add `private handlers = new Map<string, (msg: string) => void>()` to RoomManager.
2) In join(): create the handler, do `this.emitter.on('message', handler)` AND `this.handlers.set(userId, handler)`.
3) In leave(): `const h = this.handlers.get(userId); if (h) this.emitter.off('message', h); this.handlers.delete(userId);` (in addition to `users.delete`).
